# UC-17.1.25 · SonicWall High-Volume Firewall Denies from Single Source (Reconnaissance)

> **Criticality:** High &middot; **Difficulty:** Beginner &middot; **Pillar:** Security &middot; **Type:** Security &middot; **Status:** Draft

*We use network security tools to watch who accesses what, help prevent data leaks, enforce network isolation, and secure cloud and remote access.*

---

## Description

Concentrated denied flows from one source toward many destinations or ports typically indicate Internet-wide scanning, brute forcing, or a compromised internal host attempting lateral movement through the firewall.

## Value

Produces a simple, high-signal perimeter hunt that complements IDS alerts and geo-blocking policies.

## Implementation

Normalise `action` fields using the TA’s CIM aliases. Adjust the deny count threshold for your enterprise’s NAT and scanner noise profile. Enrich top sources with ASN and threat intelligence lookups; auto-submit to SOAR for containment on critical assets.

## SPL

```spl
index=netfw (sourcetype="dell:sonicwall" OR sourcetype="dell:sonicwall:firewall") earliest=-1h
| eval act=lower(coalesce(action, fw_action, act))
| eval src_ip=coalesce(src_ip, src, c_src)
| eval dest_ip=coalesce(dest_ip, dst, c_dst)
| eval dest_port=coalesce(dest_port, dst_port, dpt, service)
| where match(act,"(?i)deny|drop|block|reject")
| stats count by src_ip, dest_ip, dest_port
| where count > 75
| sort -count
| head 25
```

## CIM SPL

```spl
| tstats `summariesonly` count
  from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic
  by All_Traffic.user, All_Traffic.action, All_Traffic.src, All_Traffic.dest span=1h
| sort - count
```

## Visualization

Table (top sources), Bar chart (denies by destination port), Map (src_ip geolocation).

## Known False Positives

Access denied for users in mid-onboarding, role changes pending propagation, or VDI sessions with new device IDs.

## References

- [Splunk Connect for Syslog — Dell SonicWall](https://splunk.github.io/splunk-connect-for-syslog/main/sources/vendor/Dell/sonicwall/)
